The ACA requires insurance companies to cancel plans that do not meet certain minimum standards, such as covering essential health benefits, and many plans were canceled due to this requirement. This was a result of the 'grandfathering' provision, which allowed people to keep their existing plans if they were in place on March 23, 2010, but did not require insurance companies to continue offering them.
